heading: Recess appointments
label: 5503 Recess appointments
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 56R.S. § 1761., .July 11, 1940, ch. 58054 Stat. 751Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 56R.S. § 1761., .July 11, 1940, ch. 58054 Stat. 751Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

children.text: if the vacancy arose within 30 days before the end of the session of the Senate;

if, at the end of the session, a nomination for the office, other than the nomination of an individual appointed during the preceding recess of the Senate, was pending before the Senate for its advice and consent; or

if a nomination for the office was rejected by the Senate within 30 days before the end of the session and an individual other than the one whose nomination was rejected thereafter receives a recess appointment.

children.label: b
children.text: A nomination to fill a vacancy referred to by paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of subsection (a) of this section shall be submitted to the Senate not later than 40 days after the beginning of the next session of the Senate.
